verses.text: An angel binds 'that old serpent... Satan,' casts him into the bottomless pit 'a thousand years,' that he deceive the nations no more. Bound. Satan is chained for the thousand years.
An angel binds 'that old serpent... Satan,' casts him into the bottomless pit 'a thousand years,' that he deceive the nations no more. Bound. Satan is chained for the thousand years.
An angel binds 'that old serpent... Satan,' casts him into the bottomless pit 'a thousand years,' that he deceive the nations no more. Bound. Satan is chained for the thousand years.

The martyrs 'lived and reigned with Christ a thousand years'; 'this is the first resurrection.' Blessed are they, for the second death has no power. First resurrection. The faithful reign with Christ.

The martyrs 'lived and reigned with Christ a thousand years'; 'this is the first resurrection.' Blessed are they, for the second death has no power. First resurrection. The faithful reign with Christ.

The martyrs 'lived and reigned with Christ a thousand years'; 'this is the first resurrection.' Blessed are they, for the second death has no power. First resurrection. The faithful reign with Christ.
When the thousand years end, Satan is loosed to 'deceive the nations,' Gog and Magog, gathering them to battle. Loosed. The deceiver makes one final assault.
When the thousand years end, Satan is loosed to 'deceive the nations,' Gog and Magog, gathering them to battle. Loosed. The deceiver makes one final assault.
They compass the saints, but fire from God devours them; the devil is cast 'into the lake of fire,' tormented for ever. Final doom. Satan's end is everlasting fire.
They compass the saints, but fire from God devours them; the devil is cast 'into the lake of fire,' tormented for ever. Final doom. Satan's end is everlasting fire.
A 'great white throne'; the dead, small and great, stand, 'and the books were opened,' judged by their works. The judgment. All are judged before God's throne.
A 'great white throne'; the dead, small and great, stand, 'and the books were opened,' judged by their works. The judgment. All are judged before God's throne.
Death and hell give up their dead; 'whosoever was not found written in the book of life was cast into the lake of fire.' Second death. The unwritten perish forever.
